policies.name: Personal Data Protection Policy
policies.description: Organization-wide policy establishing principles, roles, and requirements for personal data protection in compliance with PDPA Act 709 and the 2024 Amendment Act A1727
keyContents: Policy objectives and scope
Definitions aligned with PDPA s2
Seven Principles obligations summary
Roles and responsibilities matrix
Data subject rights procedures overview
Data breach notification procedures overview
Training and awareness requirements
Compliance monitoring and reporting framework

policies.name: Data Classification Policy
policies.description: Policy defining data classification tiers (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) with handling requirements for each tier, with specific provisions for personal data and sensitive personal data under PDPA s29
policies.owner: Chief Information Security Officer / Data Protection Officer
keyContents: Data classification tiers and definitions
Classification criteria for personal data and sensitive personal data
Handling, storage, and transmission requirements per tier
Labelling and marking standards
Employee responsibilities and accountability
Declassification and reclassification procedures
Alignment with PDPA sensitive personal data categories (s29)

policies.name: Data Retention and Disposal Policy
policies.description: Policy governing the retention periods for different categories of personal data and the secure disposal of personal data when no longer necessary, in compliance with PDPA Retention Principle (s10)
policies.format: PDF/DOCX, version-controlled, with attached retention schedule
keyContents: Retention principles aligned with PDPA s10
Retention schedule by data category and processing purpose
Legal hold procedures for litigation and regulatory requirements
Secure disposal methods for physical and electronic records
Third-party disposal certification requirements
Records of disposal documentation requirements
Review triggers for retention schedule updates

policies.name: Data Breach Response Policy
policies.description: Policy establishing the organization's framework for detecting, assessing, containing, notifying, and remediating personal data breaches in compliance with PDPA s12B (mandatory breach notification) introduced by Amendment Act A1727
policies.owner: Data Protection Officer / Chief Information Security Officer
keyContents: Breach definition and classification criteria
Breach severity assessment matrix
Roles and responsibilities during breach response
72-hour notification obligation to Commissioner (s12B)
Data subject notification requirements and thresholds
Containment and remediation procedures
Post-incident review and lessons learned process
Breach register maintenance requirements

policies.name: Cross-Border Data Transfer Policy
policies.description: Policy governing transfers of personal data outside Malaysia, specifying approved transfer mechanisms, assessment requirements, and prohibited transfers, in compliance with PDPA s129 as amended by Act A1727
keyContents: Prohibition on unauthorized cross-border transfers
Approved destination countries and adequacy determinations
Transfer mechanisms: adequacy, SCCs, BCRs, consent, certification
Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) requirements
Contractual safeguards for third-country transfers
Employee responsibilities when using overseas cloud services
Ongoing monitoring of transfer arrangements
Documentation and registration requirements

policies.name: Information Security Policy
policies.description: Overarching policy establishing the organization's information security framework including technical and organizational measures to protect personal data in compliance with PDPA Security Principle (s9) and the Personal Data Protection Standards 2015
keyContents: Information security objectives aligned with s9
Risk management framework and risk appetite
Technical security controls (encryption, access control, monitoring)
Organizational security measures (training, vetting, NDAs)
Incident detection and response procedures
Business continuity and disaster recovery
Vendor and third-party security requirements
Security audit and compliance monitoring

policies.name: Access Control Policy
policies.description: Policy governing access to personal data systems and databases, establishing role-based access control, least privilege principles, and access review processes to protect data against unauthorized access in compliance with PDPA s9 and Access Principle (s12)
policies.owner: Chief Information Security Officer / IT Manager
keyContents: Role-based access control (RBAC) framework
Least privilege and need-to-know principles
Access provisioning and de-provisioning procedures
Multi-factor authentication requirements
Privileged access management
Periodic access review and recertification
Remote access and mobile device requirements
Logging and monitoring of data access

policies.name: Acceptable Use Policy
policies.description: Policy establishing permitted and prohibited uses of organizational IT systems and personal data by employees, contractors, and third parties, with specific provisions protecting data subjects' personal data
policies.owner: Chief Information Security Officer / Human Resources
keyContents: Permitted use of IT systems and personal data
Prohibited activities (unauthorized access, data exfiltration, sharing)
Personal device and BYOD requirements
Email and internet usage guidelines
Consequences of policy violations
Employee monitoring and privacy notice
Reporting obligations for suspected breaches
Annual acknowledgement requirements

policies.name: Third-Party and Data Processor Management Policy
policies.description: Policy governing the selection, engagement, oversight, and offboarding of third parties and data processors who process personal data on behalf of the organization, ensuring compliance with PDPA obligations for data processors
keyContents: Third-party risk classification framework
Due diligence requirements before engagement
Mandatory contractual clauses (data processing agreements)
Audit and inspection rights over processors
Subprocessor authorization and notification requirements
Cross-border transfer restrictions on processors
Processor security and breach notification obligations
Offboarding and data return or deletion requirements

policies.name: Direct Marketing Policy
policies.description: Policy governing the use of personal data for direct marketing purposes, including opt-in consent requirements, opt-out management, and channel-specific requirements in compliance with PDPA s40-s43
keyContents: Direct marketing consent requirements (s40)
Opt-out from direct marketing (s43) — right and obligation
Channel-specific requirements (email, SMS, telemarketing, post)
Suppression list management
Do Not Call Registry compliance
Third-party list acquisition restrictions
Record-keeping requirements for marketing consents
Children and vulnerable person protections in marketing

policies.name: Cookie and Tracking Technology Policy
policies.description: Policy governing the use of cookies, web beacons, tracking pixels, device fingerprinting, and similar technologies on organizational websites and apps, ensuring lawful use and transparency with users
policies.reviewFrequency: Annual or upon material technology change
keyContents: Categories of tracking technologies and their purposes
Strictly necessary vs. non-essential technology distinction
Consent management platform (CMP) requirements
Cookie banner design and consent collection standards
Third-party tracking technology governance
User preference centre and opt-out mechanisms
Cookie audit and scan procedures
Retention periods for tracking data

policies.name: Employee Data Protection Policy
policies.description: Policy governing the collection, processing, and protection of personal data relating to employees, job applicants, contractors, and former employees throughout the employment lifecycle
keyContents: Scope: applicants, employees, contractors, alumni
Categories of employee personal data processed
Lawful bases for employee data processing
Employee data protection notice requirements
Employee rights under PDPA (access, correction, opt-out)
Monitoring in the workplace — boundaries and disclosure
HR system access controls and data segregation
Employee data retention schedule and disposal

procedures.name: Consent Collection Procedure
procedures.description: Step-by-step procedure for collecting valid consent from data subjects across all channels (digital, physical, verbal), ensuring consent meets PDPA requirements of being freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous as defined in s2 and s6
procedures.format: SOP document with flowcharts and form templates
keyContents: Consent collection channels and method-specific requirements
Mandatory information to include with consent request (s7 notice elements)
Granular consent design — one purpose per consent
Prohibition on bundled or pre-ticked consent
Digital consent: timestamp, IP address, and version recording
Physical consent: scan, storage, and linking to data subject record
Consent for children and vulnerable persons
Consent withdrawal process and downstream notification

procedures.name: Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for receiving, verifying, processing, and responding to data subject access requests under PDPA s30, including verification of identity, search across systems, review for exemptions, and timely disclosure within the 21-day statutory period
procedures.format: SOP document with templates and flowchart
keyContents: DSAR receipt, logging, and acknowledgement within 24 hours
Identity verification requirements and methods
21-day response period and extension procedures
System search scope and methodology
Review for exemptions (national security, crime prevention, legal privilege)
Format and structure of access response
Prescribed fees and fee waiver considerations
Record-keeping of DSAR log and responses

procedures.name: Data Correction Request Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for handling data subject requests to correct inaccurate or incomplete personal data under PDPA s34, including verification of the correction request, assessment of accuracy, amendment of records, and notification to third parties
procedures.format: SOP document with request form template
keyContents: Correction request intake and logging
Identity verification of requesting data subject
Assessment of accuracy and evidentiary requirements
Amendment procedure across all relevant systems
Notification to third parties who received the inaccurate data
Dispute resolution when data controller disagrees with correction
Response to data subject within statutory period
Record-keeping of correction requests and outcomes

procedures.name: Data Breach Notification Procedure
procedures.description: Detailed procedure for detecting, assessing, containing, and notifying a personal data breach, including the 72-hour mandatory notification obligation to the Commissioner under PDPA s12B and data subject notification requirements
procedures.format: SOP with decision trees, notification templates, and RACI
procedures.owner: Data Protection Officer / Chief Information Security Officer
keyContents: Breach detection sources and initial triage
Breach severity assessment: low, medium, high, critical
Containment actions by breach type (unauthorized access, loss, ransomware)
72-hour notification to Commissioner: content requirements and portal
Criteria for mandatory data subject notification
Data subject notification content and delivery methods
Evidence preservation and forensic investigation steps
Post-incident review documentation requirements

procedures.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for conducting DPIAs for new or significantly changed processing activities involving personal data, particularly for high-risk processing, including screening, methodology, risk assessment, and DPO review
procedures.format: SOP with DPIA template and screening questionnaire
keyContents: DPIA trigger criteria — when a DPIA is required
DPIA screening questionnaire and threshold test
DPIA methodology: describe, assess, consult, implement
Risk assessment matrix for privacy risks
Identification of risk mitigation measures
DPO review and approval process
Residual risk acceptance and documentation
DPIA record-keeping and review schedule

procedures.name: Data Retention and Disposal Procedure
procedures.description: Operational procedure for implementing the Data Retention and Disposal Policy, including periodic review of data holdings, application of retention schedules, legal hold management, and secure disposal of personal data in compliance with PDPA s10
keyContents: Periodic data review cycle and ownership
Application of retention schedule by data category
Legal hold: identification, notification, and tracking
Secure disposal methods: secure delete, degaussing, physical destruction
Third-party media and hardware disposal certification
Disposal records and certificate of destruction
Cloud data deletion verification procedures
Annual disposal audit and reporting

procedures.name: Cross-Border Transfer Assessment Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for assessing and approving proposed transfers of personal data outside Malaysia, including identification of the transfer mechanism, Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA), contractual safeguards, and registration or notification to Commissioner where required under PDPA s129
procedures.format: SOP with TIA template and transfer register
keyContents: Identification of cross-border transfers (including cloud and SaaS)
Adequacy determination check against approved countries list
Selection of appropriate transfer mechanism (adequacy, SCC, BCR, consent)
Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) methodology
Standard contractual clauses drafting and execution
Binding corporate rules assessment and approval
Registration or notification to Commissioner where required
Ongoing monitoring of transfer arrangements

procedures.name: Third-Party Due Diligence Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for conducting privacy and data protection due diligence on third parties and data processors before engagement, including pre-contract assessment, data processing agreement negotiation, and periodic reassessment
procedures.format: SOP with due diligence questionnaire and DPA template
keyContents: Third-party classification (data processor, joint data controller, independent data controller)
Pre-engagement privacy questionnaire and assessment
Security and compliance certification review
Data processing agreement requirements and negotiation
Subprocessor disclosure and approval process
Annual reassessment triggers and methodology
Onsite audit rights exercise procedure
Offboarding: data return, deletion, and certification

procedures.name: Data Protection Training Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for planning, delivering, recording, and evaluating data protection training for all employees, covering mandatory awareness training for all staff and role-specific training for staff handling personal data
procedures.format: SOP with training curriculum and attendance templates
keyContents: Training needs analysis by role and data access level
Mandatory induction training for new employees (within 30 days)
Annual refresher training requirements
Role-specific training for DPO, IT, HR, legal, marketing
Training content standards aligned with PDPA principles
Training delivery methods: e-learning, classroom, scenario-based
Completion tracking and records in training register
Assessment, pass mark, and remediation for non-completions

procedures.name: Privacy by Design Review Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure embedding privacy considerations into the design and development of new products, services, systems, and business processes from inception, ensuring compliance obligations are addressed before go-live
procedures.format: SOP with privacy design checklist and approval gate criteria
keyContents: Privacy by design trigger: projects requiring DPO review
Privacy design checklist for new systems and products
DPIA trigger assessment at design stage
Data minimisation requirements in system design
Default privacy settings — privacy by default principles
DPO sign-off as pre-launch approval gate
Post-launch privacy audit and review process
Integration with SDLC and agile development workflows

procedures.name: Complaint Handling Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for receiving, acknowledging, investigating, and resolving complaints from data subjects relating to data protection matters, including escalation to the Commissioner where the complaint cannot be resolved internally
procedures.format: SOP with complaint form and response templates
keyContents: Complaint intake channels (email, form, in-person, post)
Acknowledgement within 3 business days
Complaint classification and assignment to owner
Investigation procedure and evidence gathering
Resolution options and compensation considerations
Escalation to DPO and management where required
Communication with data subject on outcome
Record-keeping and trend analysis of complaints

procedures.name: JPDP Inspection Response Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for responding to inspections, audits, and investigations conducted by the Department of Personal Data Protection (JPDP) and the Commissioner under PDPA Part VIII (s101-s109), including document production and staff coordination
procedures.format: SOP with contact list and document tracking template
keyContents: Immediate response on receipt of inspection notice
Notification of DPO, senior management, and legal counsel
Document and system preservation to prevent spoliation
Designated liaison and coordination with inspection team
Document production tracking and privilege review
Staff interview preparation and legal rights briefing
Response to information requests within required timeframes
Post-inspection remediation and follow-up reporting

procedures.name: Enforcement Notice Response Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for responding to enforcement notices issued by the Commissioner under PDPA Part IX, including assessment of the notice, legal advice, compliance actions, appeal considerations, and reporting to the Commissioner
procedures.format: SOP with escalation matrix and response template
keyContents: Receipt and immediate escalation to DPO and legal
Legal analysis of enforcement notice requirements
Assessment of appeal grounds under s83-s100
Compliance roadmap development and resourcing
Communication with Commissioner during compliance period
Evidence of compliance: documentation and reporting
Appeal Tribunal procedures if notice is contested
Post-compliance review and preventive measures

procedures.name: Data Portability Request Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for handling data subject requests to receive their personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format and to transfer that data to another data controller, where applicable under PDPA and emerging data portability rights
procedures.format: SOP with request form and data export templates
keyContents: Scope: which data is portable (data provided by data subject)
Identity verification of requesting data subject
Technical extraction and format standards (CSV, JSON, XML)
Secure transmission of exported data to data subject or third party
Exclusions: derived data, third-party data, confidential data
Response timeline and extension procedures
Logging and record-keeping of portability requests
Integration with systems for automated data extraction

procedures.name: Consent Withdrawal Procedure
procedures.description: Procedure for handling data subject requests to withdraw consent for personal data processing under PDPA s38, including cessation of processing, updating consent records, suppression list management, and notification to downstream processors
procedures.format: SOP with withdrawal form and notification templates
keyContents: Withdrawal request intake channels (as easy as giving consent)
Identity verification requirements
Assessment of alternative lawful bases for continued processing
Cessation of processing within statutory period
Suppression list update and propagation to systems
Notification to data processors and third-party recipients
Data subject confirmation of withdrawal
Exceptions where processing may continue (legal obligation, contract)

registers.name: Personal Data Inventory / Record of Processing Activities (ROPA)
registers.description: Comprehensive inventory of all personal data processing activities conducted by the organization, documenting processing purposes, data categories, data flows, retention periods, security measures, and lawful bases — serving as the master compliance record under PDPA
keyContents: Processing activity name and purpose
Data categories and special/sensitive categories
Data subjects: categories and estimated volumes
Lawful basis for processing
Data sources and collection methods
Recipient categories and processors
Cross-border transfer details and safeguards
Retention periods and disposal methods
Security measures and access controls
DPIA status

registers.name: Data Controller Registration Record
registers.description: Record documenting the organization's registration as a data controller with the Personal Data Protection Commissioner, including registration certificate details, registration class, renewal dates, and any changes notified to the Commissioner under PDPA s13-s16
registers.owner: Data Protection Officer / Legal / Company Secretary
keyContents: Registration certificate number and class
Registration date and expiry/renewal date
Registered data categories and processing activities
Notification of material changes to registration
DPO registration details and contact information
Certificate of registration (certified copies)
Correspondence with Commissioner regarding registration
History of registration renewals and amendments

registers.name: Consent Register
registers.description: Central register recording all consents obtained from data subjects, capturing the who, what, when, how, and scope of each consent to enable demonstration of compliance with PDPA s6 consent requirements and to facilitate consent withdrawal requests
registers.format: Database or consent management platform export
keyContents: Data subject identifier (pseudonymised where possible)
Consent date and timestamp
Consent version and notice version presented
Specific purposes consented to
Collection channel (web, app, paper, verbal)
Withdrawal date and method (if withdrawn)
Consent status: active, withdrawn, expired
Linked processing activities

registers.name: Data Breach Register
registers.description: Register recording all confirmed and suspected personal data breaches, including breaches that were assessed as not notifiable, to maintain a complete breach history and support compliance with PDPA s12B breach notification obligations
registers.format: Secure spreadsheet or GRC system, restricted access
registers.owner: Data Protection Officer / Chief Information Security Officer
keyContents: Breach reference number and date discovered
Nature and description of breach
Data categories and volume of records affected
Data subjects affected (categories and count)
Root cause and contributing factors
Notifiability assessment outcome and rationale
Commissioner notification date and reference (if notifiable)
Data subject notification details (if required)
Remediation actions taken and completion date

registers.name: Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) Log
registers.description: Log recording all data subject rights requests received, including access requests, correction requests, opt-out requests, withdrawal requests, and portability requests, tracking status and response timelines against statutory deadlines
keyContents: Request reference number and receipt date
Request type (access, correction, opt-out, withdrawal, portability)
Data subject identity (verified/unverified)
Statutory deadline date
Processing status and assigned handler
Extensions granted with reasons
Outcome: fulfilled, partially fulfilled, refused (with grounds)
Response date and method

registers.name: Cross-Border Transfer Register
registers.description: Register documenting all transfers of personal data to recipients outside Malaysia, recording the transfer mechanism used, recipient country, contractual safeguards, and transfer impact assessment outcomes to evidence compliance with PDPA s129
keyContents: Transfer reference and date established
Recipient country and legal system assessment
Recipient entity and role (processor, joint data controller)
Personal data categories transferred
Transfer mechanism (adequacy, SCC, BCR, consent, certification)
Transfer Impact Assessment (TIA) reference and outcome
Contractual safeguard document references
Commissioner notification reference (where applicable)
Review date and ongoing monitoring status

registers.name: Third-Party and Data Processor Register
registers.description: Register of all third parties and data processors engaged to process personal data on the organization's behalf, recording due diligence status, contract references, data categories processed, and oversight activities
keyContents: Processor name and registered address
Classification (data processor, sub-processor, joint data controller)
Services provided and personal data categories processed
Data processing agreement reference and expiry date
Due diligence assessment date and outcome
Security certifications (ISO 27001, SOC 2, etc.)
Cross-border transfer indicator and mechanism
Annual reassessment date and outcome
Offboarding completion date (for former processors)

registers.name: Training Records Register
registers.description: Register recording data protection training completions for all employees, contractors, and relevant third parties, tracking mandatory induction and refresher training compliance rates and supporting evidence of staff awareness obligations
keyContents: Employee name and employee ID
Job title and department
Training module and version
Completion date and score
Training delivery method
Certificate of completion reference
Next due date for refresher training
Non-completion status and escalation record

notices.name: General Data Protection Notice (Bahasa Malaysia and English)
notices.description: Bilingual personal data protection notice required to be provided to data subjects under PDPA s7 (Notice and Choice Principle), disclosing all mandatory information about the organization's personal data processing practices. Must be issued in both Bahasa Malaysia and English as required by the Notice and Choice Principle
notices.format: Published notice (web, physical, both), bilingual (BM + EN)
keyContents: Organization identity and contact details
Categories of personal data collected
Purposes for which personal data is processed
Disclosure to third parties and categories of recipients
Rights of data subjects (access, correction, withdrawal)
Cross-border transfer disclosure
Retention periods
Contact details for enquiries and complaints (DPO contact)
Full bilingual text: Bahasa Malaysia and English

notices.name: Employee Data Protection Notice
notices.description: Data protection notice issued to employees (and job applicants) disclosing how their personal data is collected, processed, and retained throughout the employment relationship in compliance with PDPA s7
notices.format: Document issued at onboarding; acknowledged by employee
keyContents: Categories of employee data collected (HR, payroll, performance, health)
Purposes: recruitment, employment administration, legal compliance
Lawful bases for different processing activities
Disclosure to third parties (KWSP, SOCSO, insurers, payroll processors)
Cross-border transfer disclosure where applicable
Retention periods for employment records
Employee rights and how to exercise them
DPO contact details

notices.name: Customer Data Protection Notice
notices.description: Data protection notice issued to customers at the point of data collection, disclosing how customer personal data is processed, shared, and protected throughout the customer relationship
notices.format: Physical form or digital display at point of collection
keyContents: Categories of customer data collected
Primary purposes (service delivery, billing, account management)
Secondary purposes (analytics, marketing) with opt-out
Disclosure to service partners and third parties
Cross-border transfer disclosure
Retention and disposal practices
Rights of data subjects under PDPA
Contact for access, correction, and complaints

notices.name: Website and App Privacy Notice
notices.description: Comprehensive privacy notice published on the organization's website and mobile application, disclosing data collection, processing, cookies, analytics, and user rights in compliance with PDPA s7 and the Cookie and Tracking Policy
notices.format: Web page (HTML), also available as PDF
notices.reviewFrequency: Annual or upon material technology change
keyContents: Controller identity and contact details
Data collected automatically (logs, cookies, analytics)
Data collected directly (registration, forms, purchases)
Use of cookies and tracking technologies with cookie table
Third-party integrations (payment gateways, analytics, advertising)
Data subject rights and exercise mechanism
Cross-border transfer disclosure
Last updated date and version history

notices.name: CCTV Data Protection Notice
notices.description: Notice displayed at CCTV monitoring locations informing individuals of video surveillance, the data controller's identity, the purposes of surveillance, retention periods, and data subject rights in compliance with PDPA s7 and applicable Commissioner guidance
notices.format: Physical signage at camera locations plus detailed notice at reception
keyContents: CCTV operation notification (this area is under CCTV surveillance)
Data controller name and contact details
Purposes of CCTV (security, safety, loss prevention)
Retention period for footage (typically 30 days)
Disclosure to law enforcement and security personnel
Data subject rights and how to exercise them
Reference to full privacy notice location
DPO contact details

notices.name: Consent Form Templates
notices.description: Suite of consent form templates for different purposes and channels, including digital consent forms, physical consent forms, verbal consent scripts, and parental consent forms, all designed to meet PDPA s7 notice requirements and s6 consent validity standards
keyContents: General data collection consent form (physical)
Digital consent form with purpose-specific checkboxes
Marketing opt-in consent form
Sensitive personal data explicit consent form
Research and survey participation consent form
Parental/guardian consent form for minors
Third-party data sharing consent form
Consent withdrawal form template

records.name: Consent Records
records.description: Archived records of individual consents obtained from data subjects, including the consent form version presented, timestamp, data subject identifier, and scope of consent, retained to demonstrate compliance with PDPA s6 requirements
records.format: Consent management system database; archived form scans
keyContents: Consent record unique identifier
Data subject pseudonym or identifier
Consent timestamp and channel
Notice version and content presented at time of consent
Specific consent options selected
Withdrawal record and effective date (if applicable)
Consent audit trail for lifecycle tracking
Legal hold flag (for ongoing disputes)

records.name: Data Processing Records
records.description: Operational records documenting actual personal data processing activities, including system logs, data transfer records, processing outputs, and evidence of controls applied, supporting accountability under PDPA
records.format: System logs and audit trails (automated); supplemented by manual records
keyContents: System access logs for personal data systems
Data export and transfer logs
Batch processing records
Data quality and integrity check records
De-identification and pseudonymisation activity records
Processing exception logs
System configuration change records affecting personal data
Automated decision-making records

records.name: Data Breach Investigation Records
records.description: Comprehensive records of each data breach incident investigation, including initial report, forensic findings, remediation actions, notification correspondence, and lessons learned, retained to demonstrate compliance with PDPA s12B and for future reference
records.owner: Data Protection Officer / Chief Information Security Officer
keyContents: Incident report and initial triage documentation
Forensic investigation report and root cause analysis
Evidence chain of custody records
Commissioner notification submissions and acknowledgements
Data subject notification records and delivery evidence
Third-party and processor notification correspondence
Remediation action plan and completion evidence
Post-incident review report and lessons learned

records.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) Records
records.description: Completed DPIA documents for processing activities that triggered a DPIA assessment, including the risk assessment findings, mitigation measures, DPO review notes, and approval decisions, retained as evidence of privacy by design compliance
records.reviewFrequency: Created per project; reviewed upon material change
keyContents: DPIA reference, project name, and date
Processing activity description and necessity assessment
Privacy risks identified and risk ratings
Mitigation measures recommended and implemented
Residual risk assessment after mitigation
DPO review comments and recommendations
Approval decision and approver details
Review schedule and last review date

records.name: Training Attendance Records
records.description: Records of attendance at data protection training sessions, including signed attendance sheets for classroom sessions, LMS completion certificates for e-learning, and assessment scores, retained to demonstrate the organization's ongoing staff awareness obligations
records.format: Signed attendance sheets; LMS completion certificates; HR records
keyContents: Training session date, topic, and trainer
Attendee names and employee IDs
Signed attendance confirmation
Assessment scores and pass/fail status
Certificates of completion issued
Non-attendance and remediation actions
Role-specific training completion evidence
Annual completion rate statistics

records.name: Compliance Audit Records
records.description: Records of internal and external data protection compliance audits, including audit scope, findings, risk ratings, recommended actions, management responses, and remediation progress tracking
records.format: Audit report documents with action tracker
keyContents: Audit scope and methodology
Audit program and work papers
Findings categorised by severity (critical, high, medium, low)
Root cause analysis for each finding
Management response and remediation commitments
Remediation evidence and closure documentation
Outstanding items and escalation trail
DPO and board reporting on audit outcomes

records.name: Enforcement Notice Response Records
records.description: Records relating to enforcement notices received from the Commissioner, documenting the notice content, legal assessment, compliance roadmap, actions taken, and correspondence with the Commissioner throughout the compliance period
records.format: Secure document repository, legal privilege where applicable
records.reviewFrequency: Created upon receipt; maintained through compliance period
keyContents: Enforcement notice original and receipt acknowledgement
Legal assessment memorandum
Compliance roadmap and milestones
Evidence of compliance actions taken
Correspondence with Commissioner
Appeal documents (if appeal filed)
Final compliance confirmation from Commissioner
Lessons learned and preventive measures implemented

records.name: Commissioner Correspondence Records
records.description: Records of all formal correspondence with the Personal Data Protection Commissioner and JPDP, including registration correspondence, breach notifications, inspection responses, and general regulatory communications
records.format: Document management system, retained in compliance archive
records.owner: Data Protection Officer / Legal / Company Secretary
keyContents: Registration applications and Commissioner responses
Data breach notification submissions and acknowledgements
Inspection and investigation correspondence
Enforcement notice correspondence
Code of practice consultation responses
General enquiry correspondence
DPO registration correspondence
Appeal Tribunal submissions and decisions

reports.name: Annual Data Protection Compliance Report
reports.description: Annual report to senior management and board summarising the organization's data protection compliance status, including PDPA obligations met, incidents recorded, data subject rights requests handled, training completion rates, audit findings, and planned improvements for the coming year
keyContents: Executive summary of compliance posture
PDPA obligations compliance status dashboard
Data breach incidents: count, severity, and outcomes
Data subject rights requests: volume and response performance
Training completion rates by department
Audit findings and remediation status
Third-party risk management highlights
Planned compliance improvements and resource requirements

reports.name: Data Breach Notification Report (to Commissioner)
reports.description: Formal notification report submitted to the Personal Data Protection Commissioner within 72 hours of a notifiable personal data breach under PDPA s12B, containing all prescribed information about the breach nature, scope, impact, and remediation actions
reports.format: Commissioner's prescribed notification form (online portal or paper)
keyContents: Data user identity and registration details
DPO contact details
Date and time breach was discovered
Nature and type of breach
Personal data categories and volume affected
Data subjects affected (categories and estimated number)
Likely consequences of the breach
Measures taken or proposed to address the breach
Whether data subjects have been or will be notified

reports.name: Data Breach Notification Report (to Data Subjects)
reports.description: Notification communication sent to affected data subjects following a personal data breach where there is a risk of harm to data subjects, informing them of the breach, the data affected, the potential impact, and protective actions they can take
reports.format: Email, letter, SMS, or in-app notification (as appropriate to channel)
keyContents: Clear description of the breach in plain language
Categories and approximate volume of personal data affected
Likely consequences for the data subject
Measures taken to address the breach
Protective actions data subjects can take
Dedicated helpline or contact point for enquiries
Reference to credit monitoring or identity protection services
DPO contact details and complaint rights

reports.name: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) Report
reports.description: Formal DPIA report documenting the assessment findings, risk analysis, mitigation recommendations, and DPO approval decision for a specific processing activity or system, retained as evidence of privacy by design and accountability
keyContents: Project and processing activity description
Necessity and proportionality assessment
Data flow diagram and data inventory
Privacy risks identified and risk scores
Mitigation measures and residual risk
Consultation outcomes (data subjects, stakeholders)
DPO opinion and recommendations
Final risk acceptance decision and approver

reports.name: DPO Annual Report
reports.description: Annual report prepared by the Data Protection Officer on the performance of DPO functions, the organization's compliance progress, key risks, significant incidents, and recommendations for senior management and the board, as required under PDPA s12A
keyContents: DPO activities and time allocation summary
Policy and procedure review status
Compliance monitoring and audit findings
Data breach incidents and regulatory notifications
Data subject rights request performance
Training and awareness programme outcomes
Key regulatory developments and their implications
Recommendations for board decision and resource allocation

reports.name: Third-Party Compliance Assessment Report
reports.description: Assessment report evaluating a specific third party or data processor's compliance with data protection obligations, including security posture, contractual compliance, and data handling practices, produced as part of the annual third-party oversight program
keyContents: Third-party identity and service description
Assessment scope and methodology
Security certification review (ISO 27001, SOC 2, etc.)
Compliance questionnaire findings
Contractual obligation adherence assessment
Sub-processor review
Cross-border transfer compliance
Risk rating and recommended actions
Management response and remediation commitments

contracts.name: Data Processing Agreement (DPA)
contracts.description: Formal agreement between the organization as data controller and a third-party data processor, setting out the processor's obligations, the scope of processing, security requirements, breach notification obligations, audit rights, and data return or deletion requirements in compliance with PDPA
contracts.format: Legally executed agreement (PDF), schedule to main services agreement
keyContents: Scope of processing and permitted processing activities
Processor's obligation to process only on written instructions
Confidentiality obligations of processor personnel
Security measures and minimum security standards
Sub-processor authorization and notification requirements
Data subject rights assistance obligations
Breach notification within 24 hours to data controller
Audit and inspection rights of data controller
Data return, deletion, or destruction upon termination
Cross-border transfer restrictions and safeguards

contracts.name: Data Sharing Agreement
contracts.description: Agreement governing the sharing of personal data between two or more independent data controllers, specifying the shared data categories, sharing purposes, recipient obligations, security measures, and restrictions on further disclosure or processing
keyContents: Parties and their roles (data controller identities)
Categories of personal data to be shared
Lawful basis for sharing and underlying purpose
Permitted uses of shared data by recipient
Prohibitions on onward disclosure or transfer
Security obligations and standards for shared data
Data subject rights assistance obligations
Breach notification obligations between parties
Retention and disposal of shared data
Cross-border transfer restrictions

contracts.name: Standard Contractual Clauses (Cross-Border Data Transfer)
contracts.description: Prescribed or approved standard contractual clauses for the transfer of personal data to recipients in countries without an adequacy determination, providing appropriate safeguards for cross-border transfers under PDPA s129 and the Commissioner's transfer mechanism guidance
contracts.format: Legally executed agreement with prescribed SCC schedule
contracts.reviewFrequency: Upon each new cross-border transfer arrangement
keyContents: Data exporter and data importer obligations
Categories of personal data transferred
Transfer purposes and processing instructions
Data importer security obligations
Data subject rights and third-party beneficiary rights
Data importer's local law assessment obligations
Suspension of transfers on law enforcement demand
Termination rights and data return or deletion
Governing law and dispute resolution
Sub-processing chain obligations

contracts.name: Non-Disclosure Agreement (Data Protection)
contracts.description: Confidentiality agreement incorporating specific data protection obligations for use with contractors, consultants, and professional advisers who may access personal data in the course of their engagement with the organization
contracts.reviewFrequency: Template reviewed annually; executed per engagement
keyContents: Definition of confidential information including personal data
Permitted disclosure purposes (limited to engagement scope)
Prohibition on copying, retaining, or disclosing personal data
Security obligations matching organizational standards
Obligation to report data incidents immediately
Return or destruction of personal data on engagement end
Personal liability of individual signatories
Remedies for breach including injunctive relief

contracts.name: Joint Data Controller Agreement
contracts.description: Agreement between two or more organizations that jointly determine the purposes and means of personal data processing, establishing each party's responsibilities for PDPA compliance, data subject rights handling, and liability allocation where both are data controllers for the same data
contracts.reviewFrequency: Upon establishment of joint processing arrangement
keyContents: Identification of joint data controllers and shared processing scope
Allocation of PDPA compliance responsibilities between parties
Designated lead party for data subject rights requests
Notification obligations between joint data controllers
Breach notification and response coordination
Data security responsibilities and minimum standards
Cross-border transfer compliance allocation
Liability and indemnification between joint data controllers
Transparency requirements to data subjects about arrangement
